ogTitle: Boolijah vs Boomerang Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Boolijah and Boomerang Beach.
heading: Boolijah vs Boomerang Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Boolijah, New South Wales and Boomerang Beach, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Boolijah has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Boomerang Beach (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Boolijah or Boomerang Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Boolijah has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Boomerang Beach scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Boolijah scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Boomerang Beach scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Boolijah or Boomerang Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Boolijah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
